title: phyllanthus pterocladus
intro: Phyllanthus pterocladus, also known as the Malay tea tree or Malayan tea tree, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Phyllanthus pterocladus include its ability to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis B virus, making it a potential treatment for liver diseases. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Phyllanthus pterocladus can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a decoction by simmering the roots and stems in water, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
